transcript.clean_text: first day of racing after the opening rest day on this year's welter what a day it turned out to be well a coastal route so not much climbing to be had you might think but altitude gains the wire of over two and a half thousand meters much of it of course concentrated in a category two test at the end of the day a day built for a breakaway you bet running along the coast heading towards the west and with an easterly breeze the wind was up and indeed 31 riders made the break high quality any single one of them could have won the day primus roglich happy it seemed to let the gap go out and indeed beyond a mark where that red jersey would be his surely somebody from the break or christina king or guillermo would take that jersey from him especially with this gap as we came to our final climb however the breakaway started to fracture old christian iking was keeping good pace guillaume martin was starting to fade but michael storer a bright light of this race already a stage win to his name he decided he did not want this company if it was going to come down to a sprint it wouldn't go his way so he went long on the ramps of 10 and 12 he climbed beautifully and gapped the break also thinking about a gap primos roglich he kicked off nobody could follow him it seemed well all the favourites were racked together in the chase but roglich was making a statement bernal looked secondhand adam yates not able to offer much in terms of a chase that came down to jack hague mass and lopez from movistar and then this on the descent roglich losing his back wheel on the smooth dusty road lucky to get away with it he built up a gap of around about 20 seconds that cushion he rode while he put the chain back on and indeed was joined by those trio of gc hopefuls behind him while they were to ride in together meanwhile storer was helping himself to another stage victory those had been in the break wondering what might have been and indeed issuing their own oaths at the line vance it was that led this group in it contained on christian iking who took the red jersey meanwhile edgar bernal and adam yates were trying to put a fire out roglic mass lopez hague and cuss were driving on and a roof look from roglic as mass gave him the thumbs up across the line they've done a job together essentially yates and bernal crossing the line together what a day storer the stage win odd christian iking a new leader of this race and roglich gambling it all on a descent for some minor seconds unlucky to get away with indeed just handing over the red jersey store then taking his second stage win wonderful day of racing it turned out to be odd christian couldn't quite believe it in tamashii wanti in the red though at the beginning of the day belonged to roglich he smiled as he came across the line a smile not as broad as that for old christianity a new leader on this race then second time that red jersey has been owned by intermarshy wanti after ryan tara may was in red earlier in the race bernal now four minutes and 46 down on the leader but significantly the gap between himself and roglic mass and lopez has extended likewise for adam yates so what of stage 11 well pretty much a similar scenario it could well be that a huge break goes again calorie to test at the end of the day how will it pan out we have no idea and that's what makes this race so special you
